layers.title: Captured Request Context
layers.summary: The PR adds a conversation transcript describing the requested URMA data-plane failover capability. This is review context only and does not affect runtime behavior.

layers.title: Document Client Flags
layers.summary: The client environment guide now lists the new URMA failover tuning flags after the context artifact. It documents the threshold-disable semantics and minimum sample gate for operators using the hot-reload config path.
layers.diff:
@@ -55,11 +55,21 @@
+## 客户端运行参数
+
+以下 gflag 参数可通过 `DATASYSTEM_CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH` 配置文件设置，支持热更新。
+
+| 序号 | 名称 | 默认值 | 取值范围 | 含义/用途 |
+|------|------|--------|----------|-----------|
+| 29 | `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` | `0.5` | `[0.0, 1.0]` | Client 侧 URMA 数据面成功率阈值。一个完整统计窗口的成功率严格低于该 ratio 时，client 尝试切换 worker；设置为 `0.0` 表示关闭 URMA 故障切换功能，同时清空当前窗口状态。 |
+| 30 | `urma_failover_min_sample_count` | `5` | `> 0` | 每个 `client_dead_timeout_s` 统计窗口内进行故障切换判断前所需的最小 URMA 数据面样本数。样本不足时即使成功率很低也不会触发切换，避免少量请求误触发。 |
+
+4.客户端运行参数通过 `DATASYSTEM_CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH` 配置文件设置。

layers.title: Add Tracker Library
layers.summary: Build metadata adds the tracker as a reusable client library before consumers depend on it. Both Bazel and CMake include the new source so the implementation is available across build systems.
layers.diff:
@@ -73,6 +73,21 @@ ds_cc_library(
+ds_cc_library(
+    name = "urma_success_rate_tracker",
+    srcs = [
+        "urma_success_rate_tracker.cpp",
+    ],
+    hdrs = [
+        "urma_success_rate_tracker.h",
+    ],
+    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
+    deps = [
+        "//src/datasystem/common/flags:ds_flags",
+    ],
+    alwayslink = True,
+)
@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@ list(APPEND CLIENT_SRCS
+        urma_success_rate_tracker.cpp

layers.title: Track Window Health
layers.summary: The new tracker records request-driven URMA success and failure counts into fixed windows. On window settlement it checks the hot flags, minimum sample count, strict success-rate threshold, switch-in-progress gate, and retry backoff before signaling a failover.

layers.title: Gate Switch Retries
layers.summary: After the window decision path, the tracker updates switch lifecycle and backoff state. Failed switch attempts push the next allowable switch time through a capped sequence, while healthy full windows reset the backoff.

layers.title: Expose Worker Hooks
layers.summary: The common client-worker interface gains default no-op hooks for recording URMA data-plane results, completing switch attempts, and installing a failover callback. The remote implementation owns a tracker instance and callback while non-remote implementations retain default behavior.

layers.title: Record URMA Results
layers.summary: Set/Publish style UB writes now record the real URMA write status after the transport attempt. Get records worker-to-client URMA health by treating explicit `K_URMA_ERROR` and TCP payload fallback as failed URMA data-plane samples.

layers.title: Submit Async Switch
layers.summary: Object client initialization now installs per-worker callbacks that submit asynchronous switch work when a tracker triggers. The callback validates that the reporting worker is still current before switching, avoiding stale failover requests racing with other worker changes.

layers.title: Preserve Current Worker
layers.summary: The switch reason enum adds a distinct URMA data-plane failure reason. Switch logic treats that reason like voluntary scale-down by keeping the current worker available during candidate selection, preserving TCP fallback availability if no replacement can be initialized.

layers.title: Define Runtime Flags
layers.summary: Two global flags define the URMA failover success-rate threshold and minimum sample count. They are declared for use by the tracker and added to the client-side hot-reload allowlist.

layers.title: Validate Flag Bounds
layers.summary: Validation ensures the success-rate threshold is finite and within 0.0 to 1.0, and the minimum sample count is nonzero. These guards protect hot updates from disabling failover accidentally except through the explicit threshold-zero behavior.

layers.title: Build Tracker Tests
layers.summary: Unit test build metadata adds a focused tracker test target. The target links the tracker and flags code so flag-driven threshold and sample-count behavior can be exercised directly.

layers.title: Exercise Tracker State
layers.summary: The unit tests cover full-window settlement, minimum samples, strict threshold comparison, disable-and-clear behavior, backoff, duplicate trigger suppression, hot min-sample reads, and concurrent boundary settlement. This directly targets concurrency and state-machine risks in the new tracker.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,285 @@
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, DoesNotTriggerBeforeFullWindow)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, RequiresMinimumSampleCount)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, UsesStrictThreshold)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, ThresholdZeroDisablesAndClearsWindow)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, BacksOffFailedSwitchesAndHealthyWindowResetsBackoff)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, DoesNotDuplicateTriggerWhileSwitchInProgress)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, FailedSwitchBackoffIsCapped)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, HotMinSamplesTakesEffectWhenWindowSettles)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, ShortHealthyWindowDoesNotResetBackoff)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, ZeroWindowDisablesAndClearsWindow)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, ConcurrentBoundaryTriggersOnlyOnce)

layers.title: Build Cluster ST
layers.summary: System test build metadata adds a manual KV failover suite with service discovery, flags, etcd, object-cache, and timing dependencies. This prepares the cluster-level tests that validate business continuity while failover is driven by injected URMA faults.
layers.diff:
@@ -383,3 +383,18 @@ ds_cc_test(
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "kv_client_urma_failover_test",
+    srcs = ["kv_client_urma_failover_test.cpp"],
+    tags = ["manual"],
+    deps = KV_COMMON_DEPS + [
+        "//src/datasystem/client:urma_success_rate_tracker",
+        "//src/datasystem/client:service_discovery",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/flags:ds_flags",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/kvstore/etcd:etcd_store",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/object_cache:common_object_cache",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/util:file_util",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/util:timer",
+    ],
+)

layers.title: Verify KV Failover
layers.summary: The system tests cover local UDS non-triggering, remote Set and Get failure windows, fallback success versus `K_URMA_ERROR`, switch failure preserving the current worker, healthy-window backoff reset, hot config updates, and local recovery. These scenarios validate that failover does not sacrifice foreground KV availability when TCP fallback remains usable.

summary: This PR adds client-side URMA data-plane health tracking and asynchronous worker failover with hot configuration, documentation, and tracker plus KV system-test coverage.
